SRC-01 (no photo or biometric); SRC-04 and SRC-06 (no clear face); SRC-05 (does not record device user identity).

2 AP associations: AP-04-S2 at 22:08:31 and AP-LOB-1 at 22:50:14.
No. Section 2 and Section 7 state SRC-01 records the badge identifier, not the bearer; no photo or biometric is collected.
Not closed as of 2026-04-19; the badge remained active. Closure of BD-2026-04-15-J is a separate process question being reviewed by Corporate Security IT and is not part of this packet.
Device-to-AP association — i.e., approximate location of the device (within the AP coverage zone) — and the time of association.
Activity performed on the device (association is not activity), and the identity of the device user.
No. The header explicitly states the packet is an internal investigation packet and is not a finding of misconduct.
No. Section 9.2 states the packet does not assign individual responsibility; that is a function of HR review and (if warranted) law-enforcement referral.
No. Section 5.2 states the absence of CAM-4S-12 footage during 22:00–22:45 does not establish that no movement occurred; the camera was offline by design.
No. Section 8.2 explicitly preserves the SRC-04 vs SRC-06 clothing-description conflict as unresolved; the available evidence is not sufficient to choose among scenarios (a), (b), or (c).
No. CAM-4S-12 was offline for the firmware update FW-CAM-2026-04-19 from 22:00 to 22:45; the 22:14:08 entry was not captured.
Identity of the bearer. SRC-01 records the badge identifier and the reader identity, but not who physically used the badge (no photo, no biometric).
